_comment: Clinical decision pathway for Uncomplicated UTI. Based on NICE CG149 and Pharmacy First guidance.
label: Uncomplicated Urinary Tract Infection (UTI)

questions.text: How long have you had these symptoms?
Do you have blood in your urine?
Do you have a fever (temperature above 38°C)?
Do you have pain in your back or side (loin pain)?
Are you pregnant or could you be pregnant?
Do you have a urinary catheter?
Have you taken antibiotics for a UTI in the last 3 months?
Do you have any known kidney problems or recurring UTIs (3+ per year)?
Do you have any drug allergies? If yes, please specify.

redFlags.description: Fever with loin/back pain — possible pyelonephritis (kidney infection)
redFlags.message: Your symptoms may indicate a kidney infection. Please seek urgent medical attention today.

redFlags.description: Pregnant patient with UTI symptoms — requires GP management
redFlags.message: UTI during pregnancy requires prompt GP assessment. Please contact your GP or midwife today.

redFlags.description: Catheter-associated UTI — not suitable for pharmacy treatment
redFlags.message: Catheter-related infections require GP or specialist review.

eligibilityRules.reason: Recent antibiotic use may indicate treatment resistance — GP review required
Recurrent UTIs or kidney problems require GP assessment

comorbidityModifiers.reason: Visible blood in the urine needs GP assessment to rule out causes that are not suitable for Pharmacy First treatment.
comorbidityModifiers.patientExplanationAppend: Seeing blood in your urine is a reason to see a GP, even if you also have symptoms of a urine infection.

outcomeRules.reason: Ineligible for pharmacy treatment — GP review required
Meets all criteria for Pharmacy First UTI service

pharmacyTreatmentOptions: Nitrofurantoin MR 100mg twice daily for 5 days
safetyNetAdvice: Return or seek further help if: fever develops, symptoms worsen, or no improvement within 48 hours of treatment.
